steps.title: Close the opening phrase
steps.content: The sentence begins with a long introductory phrase: "In addition to having hard hooves that can dig into nearly any groove or crack." After that phrase ends, a comma should separate it from the main clause about mountain goats' slender bodies.

steps.title: Reject broken punctuation
steps.content: A adds an unnecessary comma and "or," B leaves an extra "or" before the main clause, and C omits the comma after the introductory phrase. The correct completion finishes "groove or crack" and separates the main clause. The answer is D.
